title: agalinis heterophylla
intro: Agalinis heterophylla, also known as tall purple agalinis or woodland agalinis, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the Orobanchaceae family of flowering plants. Traditionally, Agalinis heterophylla has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Agalinis heterophylla have been found to have several health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Agalinis heterophylla can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to make poultices and salves for wound care and skin irritations.
